turnaround_reason: Enterprise AI software name has seen revenue collapse 35% in FY26 (Q4 -53% YoY) on customer pipeline weakness and execution issues; founder Tom Siebel just resumed CEO role May 8, 2026 and announced a 26% workforce reduction targeting $135M in annualized cost savings — a classic founder-led restructuring after the prior CEO's tenure failed to deliver. Stock has round-tripped from $30+ high to ~$9.
bullish_points: Strong balance sheet: $622M cash vs $60M debt provides multi-year runway through restructuring
Founder-CEO returning brings credibility and willingness to make hard cuts (26% workforce reduction announced)
Trades below cash on per-share basis (EV $832M vs $1.4B mkt cap implies negative-enterprise-value pricing — restructuring success could rerate sharply)
Strategic partnerships intact with Microsoft, AWS, Google Cloud, Baker Hughes
Near-term Q1 FY27 earnings June 3 will be first guide under Siebel — sets the bar
bearish_points: Revenue declining at accelerating rate (-46% TTM growth, -53% in Q4) — top-line collapse is unresolved
FY26 net loss of $498M, ~70% wider than FY25 — cash burn is severe relative to operations
Lawsuits referenced in recent press (securities-class-action territory after revenue miss)
Operating margin -263% — there is no operating leverage at current scale
Insider activity is net selling by CEO Siebel even ahead of his return
catalyst: Q1 FY27 earnings June 3, 2026 — first guide under returning founder-CEO; cost savings traction + Federal Defense pipeline commentary will set the tone
suggested_trade: Pass or speculative small spot position ahead of earnings. If buying, consider June 19 $10/$12 call spread — defined risk into the binary event. Skip larger size until restructuring shows topline stabilization.
key_risk: Restructuring doesn't fix revenue — the issue is product/market fit at enterprise scale against horizontal AI platforms (OpenAI, Anthropic) eating their use cases. Cost-cutting only buys time.
would_skip_if: June 3 earnings shows another sequential revenue decline; Siebel guides FY27 below $230M; net new customer count negative
